faq.question: Why does my Bosch or Siemens hood show an unrecognised `ColorTemperature` setting in the logs?
faq.answer:
A February 2024 update to the Home Connect API introduced a new enumerated setting, `Cooking.Hood.Setting.ColorTemperature`, for certain hood models. This replaced or supplemented the previous numeric `Cooking.Hood.Setting.ColorTemperaturePercent` setting with specific named values such as `neutral`.

Support for these enumerated colour temperature values was added in version `0.41.0`. If you encounter unrecognised values in your logs, ensure you are running the latest version of the plugin. Note that because the Home Connect developer portal lacks a hood simulator, this functionality is tested against physical hardware reports; if you notice issues with colour temperature control on your hood, please report them as a new issue including your appliance logs.
faq.semantic_abstract: The Home Connect API introduced an enumerated `Cooking.Hood.Setting.ColorTemperature` setting for hoods in early 2024, deviating from the previous numeric percentage-based implementation. This change requires the plugin to map specific enum values like `neutral` to HomeKit colour temperature characteristics. Affected devices include newer Bosch, Siemens, and Neff hood models that adopted the updated API specification.
